title: ericameria pulchella subsp. baileyi
intro: Ericameria pulchella subsp. baileyi, also known as Bailey's ericameria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This shrub is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems, and are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Ericameria pulchella subsp. baileyi may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, relieve pain, and promote overall well-being.
